behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real vision-model judgment of explicit content; stubbed outputs would hide misclassification failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single model decision path (image classify -> hold), not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of real explicit images through the production moderation path with the real vision model enabled, then verify they are held for review and absent from published feed/read API.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is model-output quality under adversarial text transformations; deterministic mocks cannot validate robustness to obfuscation. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text is one agentic classification task, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real-model evaluation set of hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) and assert they are consistently flagged/held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP + LLM-handoff: requires sequenced agentic decisions (image check and text check) plus final publish orchestration; false confidence is likely if either decision/handoff is stubbed. System concerns exist, but the dominant additional reality signal is multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Send benign image+text posts through the end-to-end moderation pipeline with real models, then verify round-trip visibility in the published feed and absence from hold queues.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + MULTI-STEP: latency target applies to the full real sequence including two AI stages and final decisioning; lower-level tests miss queueing/network/model variance interactions. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System-only because behavior text explicitly requires full pipeline with multiple agentic stages, not just infrastructure timing.
behaviors.test_description: Under peak-like concurrent load, submit mixed posts to the live pipeline using real model endpoints and measure p95 end-to-end submit-to-decision latency, asserting <=5s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/SYSTEM TRIAD error-path: must validate real unavailability behavior (timeouts/5xx/network failures) and externally visible retry-queue side effect; mocks can mask transport and retry semantics. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because no model-content judgment is involved when the model is unavailable.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real vision-service outage/failure mode in a staging environment, submit image posts, and verify they are placed in retry queue and never published during outage window.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP: consistency is defined across repeated real model decisions over time; this is sequential agentic behavior with potential state/caching handoff effects that stubs hide.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target time window through the real moderation service, then assert decision consistency rate meets policy threshold and no divergent publish/hold outcomes occur.
